de.uka.ilkd.key.java.reference

Interface TypeReference

  • All Superinterfaces:
    ExpressionContainer, MemberReference, ModelElement, NamedModelElement, NamedProgramElement, NameReference, NonTerminalProgramElement, PackageReferenceContainer, ProgramElement, Reference, ReferencePrefix, ReferenceSuffix, SourceElement, SVSubstitute, TypeReferenceContainer, TypeReferenceInfix
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    ProgramConstruct
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    ArrayLength, ArrayPostDecl, ConstructorCall, CreateObject, DoBreak, EnhancedForElimination, EvaluateArgs, ExpandMethodBody, ForToWhile, InitArray, InitArrayCreation, IsStatic, MethodCall, MultipleVarDecl, PostWork, ProgramSV, ProgramTransformer, SchemaTypeReference, SpecialConstructorCall, StaticInitialisation, SwitchToIf, TypeOf, TypeRef, TypeReferenceImp, Unpack, UnwindLoop

    public interface TypeReference
extends TypeReferenceInfix, TypeReferenceContainer, PackageReferenceContainer, MemberReference, NonTerminalProgramElement, SourceElement
    TypeReferences reference Types by name. A TypeReference can refer to an outer or inner type and hence can also be a MemberReference, but does not have to. A TypeReference can also occur as part of a reference path and as a prefix for types, too. As a possible suffix for types, it can have other TypeReferences as a prefix, playing the role of a TypeReferenceContainer.
